name: Ebro Reservoir Loop
name_original: Paseo por el Embalse del Ebro – circular desde Reinosa
description: Cycle 38.9 miles (62.6 km) around the Ebro Reservoir from Reinosa, a difficult touring route with 1724 feet (525 metres) of gain.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Reinosa offers diverse landscapes within the Cantabrian region of Spain. The area features the expansive Ebro Reservoir, significant elevation changes, and the source of the Ebro River at Fontibre. Cyclists can explore varied terrain, from the river valleys of the Ebro, Híjar, and Izarilla to the oak and beech forests of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. The region's topography includes both gentle paths and challenging routes with considerable climbs. Best touring cycling routes around Reinosa The most popular touring cycling route is Ebro Reservoir Loop, a 38.9 miles (62.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers picturesque views of the Ebro Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fontibre and Villacantid – circular from Reinosa, an easy 11.9 miles (19.2 km) path. This route explores the Ebro River's source region. Local touring cyclists also love the Historic Center of Reinosa – Roman Bridge loop from Reinosa, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through the historic center and past the Roman Bridge, often completed in about 4 hours 5 minutes. Touring cycling around Reinosa is defined by the Ebro Reservoir, the Ebro River valley, and the forests of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to difficult routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Reinosa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 331 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Reinosa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Reinosa?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Reinosa, offering diverse landscapes and challenges. The komoot community has explored these routes over 330 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Reinosa?
A: The region around Reinosa offers varied terrain, from the expansive Ebro Reservoir and river valleys of the Ebro, Híjar, and Izarilla, to the oak and beech forests of the Saja-Besaya Natural Park. You'll find both gentle paths and challenging routes with significant elevation gain, including mountain passes like Palombera.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy routes available. For a gentle ride exploring the source of the Ebro River, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451439/fontibre-and-villacantid-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontibre and Villacantid – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 19.2 km (11.9 miles) long.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the Reinosa area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451262/ebro-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 62.6 km (38.9 miles) route with significant elevation changes and picturesque views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, covering 70 km (43.5 miles) with substantial climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant natural and historical landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2573626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;, cycle through a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;beautiful section through the beech forest&lt;/a&gt; in Saja-Besaya Natural Park, or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3277001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palombera Pass&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning views.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Reinosa?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451262/ebro-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451439/fontibre-and-villacantid-circular-from-reinosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontibre and Villacantid – circular from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Reinosa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Reinosa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from reservoir views to forest paths, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Can I explore historical sites while cycling in Reinosa?
A: Absolutely. Reinosa has a rich history. You can cycle through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5415244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa&lt;/a&gt;, which features the Baroque Church of San Sebastián and the Plaza del Ayuntamiento. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160064" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Reinosa – Roman Bridge loop from Reinosa&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for this. Nearby, you can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3672317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Julióbriga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Reinosa?
A: While cycling is possible year-round, June and August are particularly popular months for visitors. The region's diverse landscapes mean conditions can vary, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended.

Q: Are there routes that follow rivers or offer water views?
A: Yes, the region is defined by its rivers and the Ebro Reservoir. Many routes offer stunning water views, particularly the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012451262/ebro-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The Ebro River and its tributaries, the Híjar and Izarilla, also feature prominently in the area's cycling network.

Q: Are there any routes that go through forests?
A: Yes, the Saja-Besaya Natural Park, located near Reinosa, is home to extensive forests. You can find routes that pass through a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4628486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;beautiful section through the beech forest&lt;/a&gt;, which is particularly vibrant in autumn.
tips: If you are going to visit the area, the ornithological center is the first obligatory stop. It can guide you about the most interesting things around the reservoir. They tell you about the birds that populate the Ebro Reservoir and where you can observe them (more than 70 species of waterfowl). They offer you two different routes. Each itinerary will be interpreted by the Center's specialized guide service.

The Tower of the Church of San Roque, popularly known as the Cathedral of the Fish, rises above the waters of the Ebro reservoir. With the construction of the reservoir, between 1921 and 1945, towns in the region, such as Medianedo, La Magdalena and Quintanilla, disappeared under its waters. A must visit for everyone visiting the area - the place is super nice and quiet.

It is considered the main Roman city that existed in ancient Cantabria. It has an exhibition area where the most significant objects recovered during the archaeological excavations of this Roman city and other sites in Cantabria are exhibited, explaining all of them within their historical context. You can take a guided tour of the site.

faqs.question: Is the 'Walk around the Ebro Reservoir – circular from Reinosa' suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this touring cycling route is considered challenging and requires a very good level of fitness. It covers 62.6 kilometers with over 525 meters of elevation gain, making it a hard bike ride. Some segments may be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll experience moderate cycling with some climbs, and while much of the route uses roads with lighter traffic, be prepared for some unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to push your bike. The route occasionally moves away from the immediate reservoir shore.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the 'Walk around the Ebro Reservoir – circular from Reinosa'?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts from Reinosa. You can typically find parking within Reinosa itself, which serves as a convenient base for accessing the reservoir area.

faqs.question: Are there public transport options to reach the starting point in Reinosa?
faqs.answer: Yes, Reinosa is a town with public transport connections. You can check local bus or train schedules for services to Reinosa, though specific options for transporting bicycles may vary.

faqs.question: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the Ebro Reservoir cycling route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can visit the La Población de Yuso Ornithological Center, which showcases diverse bird species. Other notable features include the Tower of the Church of San Roque (The Cathedral of the Fish) and the Julióbriga Roman City and Domus Museum. You'll also pass near the secluded Monastery of Montesclaros.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling around the Ebro Reservoir?
faqs.answer: Expect stunning and largely untouched scenery. You'll cycle along the tranquil shores of the Ebro Reservoir and through the lush mountain pastures of the Cantabrian Mountains. The route offers picturesque views of the Campoo mountains, and you might even spot eagles and vultures soaring above.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Ebro Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring colder conditions, especially in the mountains.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally welcome in many outdoor areas, this specific route is designed for touring cycling. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can keep up with the challenging 62.6 km distance and varied terrain, or consider alternative, shorter routes more suitable for pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for cycling around the Ebro Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Ebro Reservoir area is part of the Zona de Especial Protección para las Aves Embalse del Ebro (Special Protection Area for Birds). While there are no general entrance fees for cycling the route itself, it's always advisable to respect local regulations concerning protected areas, especially regarding wildlife and environmental preservation. No specific cycling permits are typically required.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Ebro Reservoir cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, Reinosa, the starting and ending point of the circular route, offers various amenities including cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. You can find places to refuel and rest before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino Olvidado (Bimón-Matamorisca), Sendero Histórico: Cantabria, Transcantábrica, Asturian Way, and Camino natural de Ebro - Cantabria.
